prompt: Create an original dub reggae track at 72 BPM in A minor, built around a stripped dub groove focused on bass, drums, echo, reverb, and empty space as arrangement devices. Build the rhythm with dry drum foundation with snare delays, rim shots, percussion drops, and sudden mute moments; use huge rounded bassline that repeats hypnotically but shifts at section turns. Feature organ stabs, guitar skank fragments, horn echoes, and tape-style feedback swells; use instrumental with occasional wordless vocal echoes. Keep melodies, riddims, horn lines, and basslines newly composed, with no named performer or existing song references. Arrange intro, groove statement, verse, pre-hook lift, chorus, dub break, second chorus, and a clean outro. Shape it for playlist-ready clarity, strong low-end pocket, clear offbeat skank, tasteful delay and reverb, and no borrowed melody. Mix with separated kick, bass, guitar, keys, horns, vocals, and dub effects.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, copied riddims, existing song titles, famous reggae hooks, label references, recognizable samples, fake patois caricature, muddy sub bass, harsh clipping, thin snare, uncontrolled feedback
